batches.title: Soft-Boiled Eggs (Monday Breakfast)
steps: Fill a pot with water, bring to rolling boil
Gently lower 8 large eggs into boiling water
Set timer for 6 minutes (soft yolk, set white)
Remove eggs to ice-cold water immediately, let cool 5 min
Gently peel shells under cool running water, store in airtight container with damp paper towel
Refrigerate until Monday morning
yields: 8 peeled soft-boiled eggs, ready to serve

day_of_assembly.Monday: Breakfast — Herb-Crusted Boiled Egg with Sourdough & Berries: toast sourdough, halve cooled boiled eggs, layer on toast with herbs, serve with berries

storage.safety_note: Hard-boiled eggs: 1 week in shell, 5 days peeled in water. Scrambled/cooked dishes: 3–4 days.

batches.title: Grilled Chicken Breasts with Mediterranean Herbs & Roasted Vegetables
steps: Pat 2.0 kg chicken breasts dry with paper towel
Mix olive oil (3 tbsp), 3 cloves minced garlic, oregano (2 tsp), thyme (1 tsp), sea salt & black pepper in bowl
Brush herb mixture generously on both sides of chicken
Heat a large grill pan or outdoor grill to high heat
Grill chicken 7-8 minutes per side until internal temp 165°F (74°C), no pink at centre
Remove to cutting board, cool to room temperature (20 min)
Slice lengthwise into 1 cm strips, portion into containers (150g Diego / 120g Diana per lunch portion × 3 portions = approx 780g total stored)
Prep roasted veg: wash & cut 500g zucchini into 1 cm batons, 400g red bell peppers into strips, 300g cherry tomatoes halved
Toss veg with 4 tsp olive oil, sea salt, black pepper in large baking tray
Roast at 200°C for 25 minutes, stirring halfway through until tender & lightly caramelised
Cool to room temperature, portion into containers (100g Diego / 75g Diana per lunch × 3 = approx 525g total)
Cook quinoa: rinse 250g dried quinoa in fine sieve, add to 500ml vegetable stock in pot, bring to boil
Cover, reduce heat to low, simmer 18 minutes until liquid absorbed & grains translucent
Fluff with fork, cool 15 minutes, portion into containers (100g Diego / 75g Diana per lunch × 3 = approx 525g total)
All containers sealed & refrigerated until Monday

day_of_assembly.Monday: Lunch — Mediterranean Grilled Chicken with Roasted Vegetables & Quinoa: reheat chicken 5 min (160°C oven), reheat veg 2 min (microwave), assemble bowl with quinoa base, top with chicken & veg, squeeze lemon
day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Lunch — Chicken Caesar Salad with Wholegrain Croutons: slice cold grilled chicken into strips, toss with fresh romaine, homemade Caesar dressing, wholegrain croutons & Parmesan shavings
day_of_assembly.Saturday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (poultry is only safe ~3 day(s); this is day 6).

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Greek Yogurt Portions (Monday & Tuesday PM Snacks)
steps: Open 1.2 kg plain, low-fat Greek yogurt
Portion into 4 airtight containers: 200g (Diego) and 150g (Diana) per container
Label containers clearly with 'PM Snack' and date
Seal & refrigerate until serving day
yields: 200g Diego portions × 2 = 400g, 150g Diana portions × 2 = 300g, total 700g Greek yogurt portioned

day_of_assembly.Monday: Pm Snack — Greek Yogurt with Walnuts & Blueberries: spoon yogurt into bowl, top with chopped walnuts & fresh blueberries
day_of_assembly.Tuesday: Pm Snack — Nectarine with Pistachios: serve yogurt separately as planned with nectarine & pistachios (alternative snack)

storage.container: sealed original packaging or airtight container
storage.safety_note: Check use-by date on packaging. Opened yogurt: 5–7 days.

batches.title: Spiced Turkey Meatballs & Brown Lentils
steps: Combine 600g minced turkey (5% fat), 2 large eggs, 80g wholegrain breadcrumbs, 4 cloves minced garlic, 2 tsp cumin, 2 tsp paprika, 1 tsp sea salt, 0.5 tsp black pepper in bowl
Mix gently with hands until just combined (do not overwork)
Roll into 36 meatballs (approx 20g each)
Line two large baking trays with parchment, arrange meatballs
Bake at 190°C for 20 minutes until cooked through (internal temp 165°F / 74°C)
Cool 15 minutes on tray, then transfer to airtight containers
Rinse 200g dried brown lentils in sieve, place in pot with 600ml vegetable stock
Bring to boil, reduce heat, simmer 25-30 min until tender but not mushy (test at 25 min)
Drain any excess liquid, cool to room temperature
Portion into containers (100g Diego / 75g Diana per lunch × 2 = approx 350g total)
Prep beets: peel 400g raw beets, slice thinly (1-2mm)
Toss with 2 tsp olive oil, sea salt, black pepper in baking tray
Roast at 200°C for 30 minutes until edges caramelised & tender
Cool, portion into containers (100g Diego / 75g Diana per lunch × 2 = approx 350g total)
All containers sealed & refrigerated until Tuesday

day_of_assembly.Tuesday: Lunch — Spiced Turkey Meatballs with Brown Lentil Salad & Roasted Beets: reheat meatballs 3 min (160°C), warm lentils 2 min, plate lentils, top with meatballs & beets, drizzle balsamic & parsley
day_of_assembly.Friday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (poultry is only safe ~3 day(s); this is day 5).

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Homemade Hummus
steps: Drain & rinse 240g cooked chickpeas (or from 1 can, drained & rinsed)
Add to food processor with 2 tbsp tahini, 2 tbsp fresh lemon juice, 2 cloves minced garlic, 2 tbsp water, 1 tsp sea salt, 0.5 tsp cumin
Blend until smooth & creamy (2-3 min), scraping sides as needed
If too thick, add 1 tbsp water at a time until desired consistency
Taste & adjust salt/lemon as needed
Portion into airtight containers: 80g (Diego) and 60g (Diana) per portion for Wednesday snack only
Top with pinch of paprika & drizzle 0.5 tsp olive oil on each
Seal & refrigerate until Wednesday
yields: 240g total hummus, portioned for 1 snack (Diego 80g + Diana 60g) with room for extra

day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Pm Snack — Hummus & Carrot Sticks: spoon prepared hummus into bowl, arrange fresh carrot & celery sticks, garnish with paprika

storage.safety_note: Cooked beans and lentils keep well. Freeze in portions for quick meals.

batches.title: Mexican Seasoned Ground Beef & Cooked Chickpeas + Cauliflower Rice + Cilantro-Lime Dressing
steps: Heat large non-stick pan over medium-high heat (no oil needed for lean beef)
Add 250g lean ground beef (5% fat), breaking apart with spoon
Cook 8-10 min until browned, rendering fat, drain any excess fat if needed
Add 2 tsp cumin, 1 tsp paprika, 1 tsp chilli powder, 2 cloves minced garlic, sea salt & pepper to taste, stir constantly 1 min until fragrant
Cool beef to room temperature, transfer to airtight container
Drain & rinse 300g cooked chickpeas (or 150g dried, pre-cooked), add to same pan, warm through (no need to cook further)
Season chickpeas with same spices if desired, cool & store in separate container (or mix with beef, as preferred)
Pulse 500g fresh cauliflower florets in food processor into rice-sized pieces (work in batches if needed)
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in large pan, add cauliflower rice in single layer, sauté 2 minutes stirring often, just to warm & dry slightly (do not overcook)
Cool, portion into containers (80g Diego / 60g Diana per dinner × 1 = approx 140g total stored; some extra for flexibility)

Make cilantro-lime dressing: blend 100g plain Greek yogurt, juice of 1 large lime (approx 3 tbsp), 20g packed fresh cilantro leaves, 1 clove minced garlic, pinch sea salt, 1 tsp olive oil until smooth
If too thick, add 1 tbsp water
Transfer to small airtight container, refrigerate
All prepped items stored separately in airtight containers, ready for Wednesday dinner assembly

day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Dinner — Mexican Lean Beef & Chickpea Taco Bowls with Cilantro-Lime Dressing: reheat beef-chickpea mix 3 min (microwave), warm cauliflower rice 2 min, build bowl with cauliflower rice base, top with warm beef-chickpea mix, raw diced red cabbage, diced avocado, cilantro-lime dressing & fresh cilantro garnish

storage.safety_note: Store in coldest part of fridge. Reheat to 74 °C before serving.

batches.title: Greek Yogurt Parfait Breakfast (Thursday AM)
steps: Portion 180g (Diego) and 140g (Diana) plain Greek yogurt into 2 glass bowls or containers, seal & refrigerate
Portion 40g (Diego) and 30g (Diana) store-bought honey granola into separate small containers, keep at room temperature (protects from sogginess until serving)
Portion 100g (Diego) and 80g (Diana) fresh strawberries, slice & store in sealed containers in fridge

day_of_assembly.Thursday: Breakfast — Greek Yogurt Parfait with Granola & Strawberries: layer into bowl — yogurt base, then granola, then strawberries, drizzle 1 tsp (Diego) or 0.75 tsp (Diana) honey on top, serve immediately

storage.container: sealed original packaging or airtight container
storage.safety_note: Check use-by date on packaging. Opened yogurt: 5–7 days.

batches.title: Herb-Baked Chicken Breast with Farro & Roasted Vegetables (Thursday Lunch)
steps: Pat 200g chicken breasts dry, place on lined baking tray
Mix 1.5 tbsp olive oil, 2 tsp lemon juice, 1 tsp dried oregano, 0.5 tsp dried thyme, 2 cloves minced garlic, sea salt & pepper
Brush mixture generously on chicken, marinate if time allows (up to 2 hrs)
Bake at 195°C for 22 minutes until internal temp 165°F (74°C), no pink
Cool to room temperature, slice into strips, portion into containers (150g Diego / 120g Diana per lunch × 1 = approx 270g total)
Rinse 70g (Diego) or 55g (Diana) dried farro, place in pot with 210ml water (3:1 ratio)
Bring to boil, reduce heat, cover, simmer 25-30 min until chewy & tender
Cool farro, portion into containers (70g Diego / 55g Diana cooked per lunch × 1 = approx 125g total)
Prep veg: cut 250g (Diego) or 200g (Diana) eggplant & zucchini into 2 cm cubes
Toss with 1 tsp (Diego) or 0.75 tsp (Diana) olive oil, sea salt, black pepper
Roast at 200°C for 25 minutes, stirring halfway, until tender & lightly golden
Cool, portion into containers (150g Diego / 120g Diana per lunch × 1 = approx 270g total)
All stored in airtight containers & refrigerated until Thursday

day_of_assembly.Thursday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (poultry is only safe ~3 day(s); this is day 4).

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Cottage Cheese & Berries Breakfast Portions (Saturday AM)
steps: Open 350g low-fat cottage cheese, portion into 2 containers: 200g (Diego) and 150g (Diana)
Cover & refrigerate until Saturday
Portion fresh mixed berries: 50g (Diego) and 40g (Diana) into small containers, refrigerate
Pre-measure honey granola: 35g (Diego) and 25g (Diana) into small containers, keep at room temperature in sealed bags

day_of_assembly.Saturday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (dairy is only safe ~5 day(s); this is day 6).

storage.container: sealed original packaging or airtight container
storage.safety_note: Check use-by date on packaging. Opened yogurt: 5–7 days.
